description: A plain CREATE INDEX on a large production table takes a SHARE lock and blocks all writes until the build finishes.
body: Failure mode: CREATE INDEX (without CONCURRENTLY) acquires a lock that allows reads but blocks INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for the entire build, which on a big table can be minutes of write downtime. Detection signals: writers stall and queue during a deploy; pg_stat_activity shows sessions blocked on the index relation; the migration ran a bare CREATE INDEX. Do not run plain index builds on hot tables. Safer fallback: use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY outside a transaction, clean up any INVALID index if it fails, and reserve plain builds for empty tables or maintenance windows.
